title: Warp Commerce Model — Money
description: Typed monetary values (Primitive 2: Value -> ValueForm -> Money). Money ALWAYS carries its currency; there is no amount without a denomination. MoneyBreakdown decomposes a total into typed components that must sum to the total (extends Invariant 1).

CurrencyCode.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. "MAD", "EUR", "USD"). Open string: also admits CurrencyCode::Custom denominations such as loyalty points and store credits (e.g. "PTS"). The TypeScript generator re-applies the literal-suggestion-plus-open-string form.

Money.description: A monetary value. `currency` is required, always — Decimal alone is not valid Money. This makes accidental currency mixing impossible to express.

amount.description: The numeric amount, in major currency units.

MoneyComponentKind.description: The kind of a single component in a MoneyBreakdown.

MoneyComponent.description: One typed component of a monetary total. A Discount component carries a negative amount (it reduces the total). Tax components may carry a tax_rate and jurisdiction.

tax_rate.description: Optional fractional tax rate (0.0 to 1.0) for Tax components.

jurisdiction.description: Optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 jurisdiction for Tax components, e.g. "MA".

MoneyBreakdown.description: A structured decomposition of a monetary total into typed components (Base / Tax / Discount / Shipping / Surcharge / Tip / Adjustment). CORE from v1. INVARIANT (see behavior/invariants.json, I-1 / rule money_breakdown_sum): the components MUST sum to `total` in the SAME currency (Discounts subtract). All component currencies and the total currency must be identical. This is the structural sum rule that extends Invariant 1 (Value Conservation). Money used anywhere a total may decompose MAY be expressed as plain Money OR as MoneyBreakdown; breakdown is optional so existing plain-Money usage stays valid.

MoneyOrBreakdown.description: Either a plain Money value or a structured MoneyBreakdown. Use where a total MAY decompose (e.g. a CommitmentSubject requested value) while keeping plain-Money usage valid.
